text: Hello and welcome to 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil and today we have Hannah joining us again from BBC podcast What In The World. Welcome, Hannah.
zh: [1] 我是Phil，今天BBC播客节目《What In The World》的Hannah再次加入我们。欢迎你，Hannah。

text: Hey Phil. It is so great to be back here.
zh: [2] 嘿，Phil，能再次回到这里真是太好了。

text: So, I'm wondering, do you do things that you think are good for the planet?
zh: [3] 我想问一下，你会做一些你认为对地球有益的事情吗？

text: Oh, I try, but I don't think I do enough.
zh: [4] 哦，我努力去做，但我觉得做得还不够。

text: So maybe I cycle more than I drive and I try to recycle things, but I should be doing a lot more.
zh: [5] 我骑自行车的次数可能比开车多，我也尽量做回收利用，但我应该做得更多。

text: But some people think that to deal with climate change, we can't just rely on individuals.
zh: [6] 但有些人认为，要应对气候变化，我们不能只依靠个人的努力。

text: Phil, it's not up to you and me. We need to actually change the way our economy works.
zh: [7] Phil，这不只是你我能决定的事。我们真正需要改变的是我们的经济运作方式。

text: And this is an idea called degrowth, and we've been investigating it at What In The World, and we're going to learn a bit more about it today.
zh: [8] 这就涉及一个叫做“去增长”的概念，我们在《What In The World》节目中一直在探讨它，今天我们也来多了解一些。

text: But first, our quiz question. World leaders promised in 2015 to limit global temperature rises,
zh: [9] 不过首先来看我们的小测验问题。2015年，世界各国领导人承诺要限制全球气温上升，

text: but what was the limit they agreed to? Was it a 1 .5 degrees Celsius,
zh: [10] 那么他们商定的限制是多少呢？是a. 1.5摄氏度，

text: b 2 .5 degrees Celsius, or c 3 .5 degrees Celsius?
zh: [11] b. 2.5摄氏度，还是c. 3.5摄氏度？

text: I think I know this one, Phil. I think it's 1 .5 degrees Celsius.
zh: [12] Phil，我想我知道答案，应该是1.5摄氏度。

text: OK, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: [13] 好的，我们稍后会在节目中揭晓答案。

text: So, Hannah, you've been researching degrowth. What does that mean?
zh: [14] 那么Hannah，你一直在研究“去增长”，这到底是什么意思呢？

text: Well, Phil, traditionally governments have always wanted to grow the economy.
zh: [15] Phil，传统上各国政府一直都希望经济增长。

text: More money, more jobs, more stuff.
zh: [16] 更多的钱、更多的就业机会、更多的物质产出。

text: But degrowth is a different way of thinking about things.
zh: [17] 但“去增长”提供了一种不同的思考方式。

text: Some people say that to tackle the climate crisis, we need to stop our economies from growing.
zh: [18] 有人认为，要应对气候危机，我们需要停止经济增长。

text: We need to stop using and making more and focus on using fewer of the Earth's natural resources.
zh: [19] 我们需要停止过度使用和生产，转而专注于减少对地球自然资源的消耗。

text: The people who support the idea of degrowth are often called degrowthers.
zh: [20] 支持“去增长”理念的人通常被称为“去增长主义者”。

text: Right. OK, so degrowthers argue we should make and use less stuff.
zh: [21] 好的，也就是说，去增长主义者主张我们应该少生产、少消费。

text: Yes, mostly. It's a little bit more complex than that.
zh: [22] 是的，大体如此，不过实际情况要复杂一些。

text: And I spoke to BBC journalist Álvaro Álvarez, who told me more about the idea.
zh: [23] 我采访了BBC记者Álvaro Álvarez，他向我详细介绍了这个理念。

text: So, degrowth is not saying that we need to reduce everything.
zh: [24] “去增长”并不是说我们需要减少一切。

text: It's saying that we need to decrease some elements of our production that are not beneficial.
zh: [25] 而是说我们需要减少那些没有益处的生产环节。

text: An example of those elements would be fast fashion,
zh: [26] 这类环节的例子包括快时尚、

text: private jets, the beef and meat industry. So it's not about decreasing everything.
zh: [27] 私人飞机，以及牛肉和肉类产业。所以并不是要减少所有的东西。

text: Alvaro says that de -growthers believe we should reduce some elements
zh: [28] Alvaro说，去增长主义者认为我们应该减少生产中的某些环节。

text: of production. An element of something is a part of something.
zh: [29] “element”（要素）指的是某事物的一部分。

text: And according to Alvaro, these elements of production are not believed to be
zh: [30] 根据Alvaro的说法，这些生产环节被认为并不

text: beneficial. If something is beneficial, it's useful or good.
zh: [31] 有益。如果某事物是“beneficial”（有益的），那就是有用或好的。

text: One example of a kind of production that de -growthers disagree with is fast fashion.
zh: [32] 去增长主义者反对的一种生产类型的例子就是快时尚。

text: Fast fashion refers to clothes that are made very cheaply and quickly,
zh: [33] 快时尚指的是那些生产成本低、制作速度快的服装，

text: so that customers can afford to buy new outfits more often.
zh: [34] 目的是让消费者能够更频繁地购买新衣服。

text: Exactly. And before we go any further, many economists do not think that degrowth is a good idea.
zh: [35] 没错。在我们继续讨论之前，要说明的是，许多经济学家并不认为“去增长”是个好主意。

text: Some of them argue that it would create more poverty,
zh: [36] 有些人认为这会导致更多的贫困，

text: and some economists believe degrowth would actually make it harder to solve the climate crisis.
zh: [37] 还有一些经济学家认为，“去增长”实际上会让解决气候危机变得更困难。

text: For example, Professor Sam Fankhauser, an economist at Cambridge University in the UK,
zh: [38] 例如，英国剑桥大学的经济学家Sam Fankhauser教授

text: spoke to you on What in the World? And he argues that degrowth could limit innovation.
zh: [39] 曾在《What In The World》节目中接受采访。他认为“去增长”可能会限制创新。

text: I think degrowth underestimates the power of innovation.
zh: [40] 我认为“去增长”低估了创新的力量。

text: Degrowth underestimates the need for investment that we need in new technology and in new solutions.
zh: [41] “去增长”低估了我们在新技术和新解决方案上所需的投资力度。

text: and degrowth underestimates the social sort of feasibility of what they propose.
zh: [42] 同时，它也低估了其所提出方案在社会层面上的可行性。

text: So Professor Fankhauser thinks that degrowth underestimates many things, such as innovation.
zh: [43] 所以Fankhauser教授认为，“去增长”低估了许多方面，比如创新。

text: If you underestimate something, you don't understand the true importance or size of it.
zh: [44] 如果你“underestimate”（低估）某事物，就是没有充分认识到它真正的重要性或规模。

text: Another factor that Professor Fankhauser says is underestimated by degrowthers is the feasibility of social change.
zh: [45] Fankhauser教授提到的另一个被去增长主义者低估的因素，是社会变革的可行性。

text: Feasibility means the possibility that something can be achieved. It comes from the adjective feasible.
zh: [46] “Feasibility”（可行性）指的是某事能够实现的可能性，它源自形容词“feasible”（可行的）。

text: For example, I could say that I think it's feasible for us to finish this podcast in six minutes.
zh: [47] 举个例子，我可以说我认为我们有可能在六分钟内完成这期播客。

text: I think we can do it. Well, Phil, we're going to have to get a move on.
zh: [48] 我觉得我们能做到。好了，Phil，我们得抓紧时间了。

text: I think it's time to hear the answer to your quiz question. I asked how much world leaders agreed to limit global temperature rises
zh: [49] 我想是时候公布你那个小测验问题的答案了。我之前问的是，世界领导人商定要将全球气温上升限制在多少度以内。

text: to. The answer is 1 .5 degrees Celsius,
zh: [50] 答案是1.5摄氏度，

text: which scientists believe is needed to stop the worst impacts of climate change.
zh: [51] 科学家认为，这是防止气候变化造成最严重影响所必需的限度。

text: I got it right! OK, so let's recap some of the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: [52] 我答对了！好了，让我们来回顾一下今天学到的一些词汇，

text: starting with degrowth, which is the economic idea that we should use and make less stuff.
zh: [53] 首先是“degrowth”（去增长），这是一种经济理念，主张我们应该少生产、少消费。

text: An element of something means a part of something. Beneficial means useful or good.
zh: [54] “element of something”指某事物的一部分。“beneficial”意思是有用的或有益的。

text: Fast fashion refers to clothes that are made cheaply and quickly so that people can buy them more often.
zh: [55] “fast fashion”（快时尚）指的是制作成本低、速度快的服装，方便人们更频繁地购买。

text: And if you underestimate something, you don't understand the true importance or size of it.
zh: [56] 如果你“underestimate”（低估）某事物，就是没有真正认识到它的重要性或规模。

text: The feasibility of something is the likelihood that it can be achieved.
zh: [57] 某事物的“feasibility”（可行性）指的是它能够实现的可能性。

text: We can also describe something as feasible or unfeasible.
zh: [58] 我们也可以用“feasible”（可行的）或“unfeasible”（不可行的）来描述某事物。

text: We can do it or we can't do it.
zh: [59] 也就是说，我们能做到，或者做不到。

text: Phil, once again our six minutes are up, but there is just a little bit more time for me to tell you more about my podcast, What In The World?
zh: [60] Phil，我们的六分钟又到时间了，不过还有一点时间让我再多介绍一下我的播客节目《What In The World》。

text: We have a new episode every single weekday and we talk about the news, trending topics.
zh: [61] 我们每个工作日都会推出新一期节目，讨论新闻和热门话题。

text: In fact, it's a great place to come and learn some new vocabulary about things that are happening in the world.
zh: [62] 事实上，这是一个学习与世界时事相关新词汇的好地方。

text: Yes. If you've found this episode interesting, try watching What In The World on YouTube with subtitles to practise your English.
zh: [63] 是的，如果你觉得这期节目有意思，可以去YouTube上观看带字幕的《What In The World》，练习你的英语。

text: There's a link in the notes for this episode. And don't Don't forget to try the quiz and worksheet at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [64] 本期节目说明中附有链接。别忘了到bbclearningenglish.com尝试小测验和练习题。

text: Phil, thank you so much for having me again on 6 Minute English. Bye for now. Bye.
zh: [65] Phil，非常感谢你再次邀请我参加《六分钟英语》。再见啦。再见。
